konnect.description:
Base Konnect configuration.

Contains all the necessary information to connect to Konnect,
including TLS configuration for mTLS authentication.

region.description: The Konnect region to connect to (e.g., "us", "eu")

gateway_cluster_id.description: The Gateway Cluster ID (also known as Control Plane ID)

api_request_timeout.description: Timeout for API requests to Konnect (in seconds)

insecure_skip_verify.description: Skip TLS verification (insecure, for testing only)

client_key.description: Client private key for mTLS (PEM format)

client_key_path.description: Path to client private key file

config_poll_interval.description:
The interval at which to poll for configuration updates

Environment variable: `KEG__CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL`

drain_duration.description:
Duration to wait for existing connections to drain. It is the time between

Environment variable: `KEG__RUNTIME__DRAIN_DURATION`

shutdown_timeout.description:
Timeout for graceful shutdown. It is the time between sending the shutdown
signal and forcefully terminating the process.

Environment variable: `KEG__RUNTIME__SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT`

health_listener_address_port.description:
Address and port for the health listener.

Environment variable: `KEG__RUNTIME__HEALTH_LISTENER_ADDRESS_PORT`

log_flags.description:
Configure the log level. Possible levels are: "trace", "debug", "info", "warn", "error"

The format is a comma-separated list of log levels for different modules, e.g., `info,keg=debug,my_module=trace`
If only a single level is provided, it applies to all modules.

Environment variable: `KEG__OBSERVABILITY__LOG_FLAGS`

log_format.description:
Configure the log format.

Environment variable: `KEG__OBSERVABILITY__LOG_FORMAT`

metrics_rollup_allow_map.description:
Configure the metrics rollup allow map, this is to avoid high cardinality metrics for example `messaging.operation.name`, `messaging.destination.name` or `messaging.destination.partition.id`.
The format is `<label_key>=<allowed_value1>,<allowed_value2>|<label_key2>=<allowed_value3>,<allowed_value4>`
Non-allowed values will be mapped to "other"

Environment variable: `KEG__OBSERVABILITY__METRICS_ROLLUP_ALLOW_MAP`

policy_errors_info_log_interval.description:
Interval for INFO-level logging of policy errors (time-based sampling).
Set to "0s" to disable.

Environment variable: `KEG__OBSERVABILITY__POLICY_ERRORS_INFO_LOG_INTERVAL`

enable_debug_endpoints.description:
Enables debug endpoints:

- /debug/pprof/allocs endpoint
  Additionally, you need to enable jemalloc profiling like this `MALLOC_CONF="prof:true,prof_active:true,lg_prof_sample:19"`

Environment variable: `KEG__ENABLE_DEBUG_ENDPOINTS`

Secret.description:
Wrapper for sensitive values that redacts content in debug output.

This type wraps the `secrecy` crate's `SecretString` and adds `JsonSchema`
support for configuration schema generation. The underlying value is
zeroized on drop for memory protection.

OtelConfig.description:
OTLP exporter configuration for all signals.

Follows the [OpenTelemetry OTLP exporter specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/sdk-configuration/otlp-exporter/).
Signal-specific environment variables (e.g., `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_*`) take
precedence over base variables (`OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_*`).

Environment variables:
- Base: `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TIMEOUT`
- Traces: `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT`
  - Sampler: `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER`, `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER_ARG`
- Metrics: `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_ENDPOINT`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_PROTOCOL`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_METRICS_TIMEOUT`
- Logs: `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_ENDPOINT`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_PROTOCOL`, `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_LOGS_TIMEOUT`

traces.description: OTLP exporter and sampler configuration for traces.

OtelTracesConfig.description: Trace signal configuration: OTLP exporter settings plus sampler configuration.

endpoint.description:
OTLP endpoint URL.

For HTTP protocols, should include the full path (e.g., `http://localhost:4318/v1/{signal}`).
For gRPC, should be the base URL (e.g., `http://localhost:4317`).

If not set, the signal is considered disabled.

timeout.description: Request timeout for the OTLP exporter.

headers.description:
Additional headers to send with OTLP requests.

Headers are specified as key-value pairs.

OtlpProtocol.description:
OTLP transport protocol.

Wraps `opentelemetry_otlp::Protocol` with serde and schema support.

TracesSampler.description:
Trace sampler strategy.

Corresponds to the `OTEL_TRACES_SAMPLER` environment variable defined in the
[OpenTelemetry SDK environment variables specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/#general-sdk-configuration).

Currently only `parentbased_traceidratio` is supported.
This field exists for spec compliance. Support for additional samplers may be added
in future releases.

OtelMetricsConfig.description:
Metrics signal configuration: OTLP exporter settings for metrics.

All fields from [`OtelSignalConfig`] are available via `#[serde(flatten)]`.

endpoint.description:
OTLP endpoint URL.

For HTTP protocols, should include the full path (e.g., `http://localhost:4318/v1/{signal}`).
For gRPC, should be the base URL (e.g., `http://localhost:4317`).

If not set, the signal is considered disabled.

timeout.description: Request timeout for the OTLP exporter.

headers.description:
Additional headers to send with OTLP requests.

Headers are specified as key-value pairs.

OtelLogsConfig.description:
Logs signal configuration: OTLP exporter settings for logs.

All fields from [`OtelSignalConfig`] are available via `#[serde(flatten)]`.

endpoint.description:
OTLP endpoint URL.

For HTTP protocols, should include the full path (e.g., `http://localhost:4318/v1/{signal}`).
For gRPC, should be the base URL (e.g., `http://localhost:4317`).

If not set, the signal is considered disabled.

timeout.description: Request timeout for the OTLP exporter.

headers.description:
Additional headers to send with OTLP requests.

Headers are specified as key-value pairs.
